prompt_text:
Analyze the following java source code repository for violations of the Interface Segregation Principle (ISP).

## Principle Definition
Clients should not be forced to depend on interfaces they do not use. When a class implements an interface but leaves some methods empty or raises NotImplementedError, it violates ISP. Large interfaces that force implementing classes to provide unneeded methods violate ISP.

## What to Look For
- Abstract base classes or interfaces with too many methods
- Classe...

findings.description: JsonGenerator is a 'fat' interface/abstract class forcing implementations to support unrelated concerns.
findings.reasoning: The JsonGenerator class combines structural writing (writeStartArray, writeEndObject), scalar writing (writeNumber, writeString, writeBoolean), binary data handling (writeBinary), and high-level object serialization (writePOJO, writeTree). Implementations like binary format generators are forced to implement methods that may not be applicable or efficient for their format, violating the Interface Segregation Principle.
